channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: First Asian edition of Eurovision to be held in Bangkok later this year
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest is set to launch its first-ever Asian edition in Bangkok, Thailand, later this year. The event will feature at least ten countries, including Thailand, South Korea, the Phi...

item.title: Independent watchdog investigates Surrey police-involved shooting
item.description: Authorities in Surrey, British Columbia, are under scrutiny following a police-involved shooting that left a man injured. The Independent Investigations Office (IIO), a provincial oversight body respo...

item.title: Tanzania maintains B+ credit rating with steady growth and manageable debt
item.description: Tanzania has kept its B+ credit rating with a stable outlook, according to Fitch Ratings, reflecting steady economic growth and manageable debt, but still facing structural challenges. Analysts say th...

item.title: DOJ and Ombudsman handle numerous flood control project cases, says Palace
item.description: Malacañang clarified that the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the Office of the Ombudsman are currently handling a large volume of cases related to alleged irregularities in flood control projects. Ac...

item.title: Ishaq Dar visits China to discuss Pakistan’s mediation role in US-Iran dialogue and regional peace efforts
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar embarked on a one-day trip to China on Tuesday for in-depth consultations on recent regional developments. According to the Foreign Office, the mai...

item.title: Canadian and global markets show modest gains amid ongoing Iran conflict uncertainty
item.description: Global equity markets posted gains on Tuesday as investors balanced optimism over potential diplomatic progress in the Iran conflict with concerns about rising inflation and recession risks. Wall Stre...

item.title: Key African News Updates Including Uganda Jet Sighting and Kenyan Marathon Ban
item.description: On March 31, 2026, several significant events unfolded across Africa. In Uganda, a presidential jet was spotted in Savannah, Georgia, with no official explanation, raising questions about First Lady J...

item.title: Manila increases financial aid for jeepney drivers amid rising fuel prices
item.description: The Manila local government has increased financial assistance for jeepney drivers participating in the 'libreng sakay' program to P3,500 per day, up from the previous P3,000. This move aims to help d...

item.title: Gunmen attack Angwa Rukuba community in Jos North, Plateau, as state government impose 48-hour curfew
item.description: For the Jos North area of Plateau State, wahala burst as gunmen attack Angwa Rukuba community on Sunday night, 29th March 2026. Plenty residents, including some students, lose their lives for the atta...

item.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie to miss Easter service following father's legal issues
item.description: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie will not attend the traditional Easter Sunday service with the royal family at St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle, following the recent arrest of their father, Andrew M...

item.title: Pipeline blast disrupts gas supply across several districts of Balochistan
item.description: Gas supply in Quetta and several nearby districts of Balochistan has been disrupted after an 18-inch main gas pipeline was blown up by unidentified armed men in the Akhtarabad area on the outskirts of...

item.title: AXA Health International partners with local insurers to offer global healthcare in Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam – AXA Health International, a major global insurance company, has officially entered the Tanzanian market by partnering with Alliance Insurance Corporation Limited and MIC Global Risks T...

item.title: Political debate intensifies over Gauteng’s response to Foot-and-Mouth Disease outbreak
item.description: Political tensions are rising in Gauteng as the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (FMD) outbreak continues to spread, causing major losses for farmers and threatening food security. The Democratic Alliance (DA) ...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Emphasises Importance of Modern Payment Systems for Digital Economy
item.description: Tanzania is pushing forward its digital economy, and Bank of Tanzania (BoT) is making it clear that reliable and inclusive payment systems are key for economic growth and financial inclusion. Deputy G...

item.title: Rajasthan Board Class 12 Results 2026 Released on Official Portal
item.description: The Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (RBSE), headquartered in Ajmer, has officially released the Class 12 examination results for the year 2026. Students who appeared for the exams can now check...

item.title: Distribution of 5 Million New Gen Z Voters Across Kenyan Counties Ahead of 2027 Polls
item.description: According to analysis of official population data, Kenya is set to register approximately 5.1 million new Gen Z voters ahead of the 2027 General Election. These youths, born between 2006 and 2009, wil...

item.title: French court rejects Cardiff City's compensation claim over Emiliano Sala's death
item.description: A French court has dismissed Cardiff City Football Club's claim for more than £100 million in compensation following the death of Argentine footballer Emiliano Sala. Sala, aged 28, and pilot David Ibb...

item.title: US backs Israel’s legislative autonomy amid controversy over death penalty for Palestinians
item.description: The United States reaffirmed its respect for Israel’s sovereign right to determine its own laws after the Israeli parliament passed legislation introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted...

item.title: Former Tanzanian MP Selemani Bungara Dies After Long Illness
item.description: Former Tanzanian Member of Parliament Selemani Bungara, famously known as Bwege, passed away on March 30, 2026, in Dar es Salaam while undergoing treatment at EM Hospital. He had been struggling with ...

item.title: Nigerian Army Secures Key Towns, Repels Insurgent Attacks in March 2026
item.description: For March 2026, the Nigerian Army show strong courage and resilience as dem dey fight insurgency for North-East Nigeria. Troops celebrate Eid-el-Fitr with Ngoshe residents after securing Forward Opera...
